data.title: Paradigm Challenges Regulatory Efforts on Bitcoin Mining Energy Use
data.description: Paradigm, a cryptocurrency investment firm, has released a report questioning regulatory actions aimed at Bitcoin mining due to concerns over energy consumption. According to NS3.AI, the firm argues that Bitcoin mining predominantly takes place during off-peak hours when electricity costs are lower, which aids in stabilizing the power grid. Paradigm asserts that Bitcoin mining mitigates significant consumer electricity usage instead of imposing excessive strain on it.

data.title: Bus Accident in São Paulo Results in Multiple Casualties
data.description:
A bus accident in São Paulo, Brazil, has resulted in at least six fatalities and 45 injuries. According to RTHK, the incident occurred early Monday morning when a bus carrying farm workers experienced a tire blowout. This caused the vehicle to lose control, veer off the road, and overturn on the route connecting the cities of Ocauçu and Marília in São Paulo state. The injured have been transported to hospitals for treatment.

The São Paulo State Public Security Secretariat stated that police are in the process of identifying the victims and other individuals involved in the accident. The cause of the incident is currently under investigation.

data.title: Market Dynamics Shift as Institutional Discipline Takes Hold in Crypto
data.description: Bitcoin and the broader cryptocurrency market are experiencing a transition from retail-driven speculative trading to a more disciplined approach led by institutional investors. According to NS3.AI, this shift is accompanied by tightening regulations, which are enhancing market structure and reducing volatility. Capital is increasingly consolidating into assets that comply with governance standards. WisdomTree emphasizes that the primary concern now is how to responsibly integrate cryptocurrencies into investment portfolios as market dynamics continue to evolve.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia, U.S., and Ukraine to Discuss Territorial Issues in Geneva Talks
data.description:
Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov announced that the trilateral talks between Russia, the United States, and Ukraine, scheduled for Tuesday in Geneva, Switzerland, will address broader issues, including territorial matters. According to RTHK, Peskov noted that the Russian delegation has expanded compared to previous meetings held in Abu Dhabi, UAE. The delegation is led by Presidential Assistant Vladimir Medinsky and includes Deputy Foreign Minister Andrey Rudenko and Chief of the General Staff's Main Intelligence Directorate, Igor Kostyukov. Before departing, the delegation received detailed instructions from President Vladimir Putin.

Peskov also mentioned that the Russia-U.S. Economic Working Group will convene during the Geneva talks. Dmitry Dmitriev, the President's Special Representative for Foreign Investment and Economic Cooperation, will participate in the meeting.

data.title: XRP Faces Potential Correction Amid Whale Selling Pressure
data.description: XRP's price has experienced a nearly 10% decline recently, driven by significant selling activity from large whale holders. According to NS3.AI, this selling pressure persists despite efforts from long-term investors to absorb the excess supply. Technical analysis indicates a bearish rising wedge pattern, suggesting a possible 26% correction if the support level near $1.26 fails to hold. The future direction of XRP's price is contingent on whether holders can effectively counteract the whale selling or if a breakdown below critical support levels pushes XRP toward $1.

data.title: Hedge Funds Increase Investments in Asian Markets Amid AI Optimism
data.description: Emerging and developed Asian markets experienced significant net-buying by hedge funds last week, marking the largest influx since Goldman Sachs Prime Services trading desk began monitoring this data in 2016. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the region's appeal due to growing optimism surrounding AI infrastructure firms. This surge in investment reflects a broader trend of confidence in the technological advancements and economic potential within Asia. The increased interest from hedge funds underscores the strategic importance of the region in the global financial landscape.

data.title: Trump Criticizes California-UK Clean Energy Agreement
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has sharply criticized California's recent clean energy agreement with the United Kingdom, according to Jin10. Trump condemned California Governor Gavin Newsom and cautioned UK leaders against collaborating with the Democratic governor. In an interview, Trump remarked, "The UK is already in enough trouble by getting involved with 'Gavin Newsom trash.'" He used a derogatory nickname for Newsom, stating, "Gavin is a loser, and everything he touches turns to trash. His state is finished, and his environmental efforts are a disaster." Trump added that Newsom's signing of such agreements is "inappropriate," and it is "inappropriate" for the UK to engage with him.

Trump's comments came shortly after Newsom signed a memorandum of understanding in London with UK Energy Minister Miliband, pledging cooperation in clean energy technologies such as offshore wind power. The agreement also aims to expand UK companies' access to the California market and foster collaboration between research institutions across the Atlantic.

data.title: Has Bitcoin Entered a Bear Market? This Chart Suggests So
data.description:
Bitcoin has closed below its 50-week Simple Moving Average, the same structural trigger that preceded bear markets in 2014, 2018, and 2022. Bitcoin is currently trading near $68,400, down more than 40% from its October 2025 all-time high around $126,000.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/has-bitcoin-entered-a-bear-market-this-chart-suggests-so/

data.title: U.S. Crypto Mining Industry Addresses Legislative Concerns Over Energy Use
data.description: The U.S. crypto mining sector is actively addressing legislative initiatives aimed at curbing data and mining operations due to concerns over high energy consumption. According to NS3.AI, industry advocates are emphasizing that mining could help stabilize energy volatility instead of worsening it. They are engaging with policymakers to dispel misconceptions and highlight the potential benefits of mining for the energy grid.

data.title: CZ Reveals Why He Hesitated On BTC – And Regretted It
data.description:
Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) says he spent six months studying Bitcoin (BTC) before investing, only to believe he had already missed the opportunity. In a recent podcast, CZ revealed he first discovered BTC in 2013 when it was around $70, but waited until prices approached $1,000 before entering the market. com
source: https://techgaged.com/cz-reveals-why-he-hesitated-on-btc-and-regretted-it/

data.title: Third Point Invests in Indra Sistemas, Supports Acquisition Plan
data.description: Third Point has acquired a stake in the Spanish technology and defense firm Indra Sistemas. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the investment firm is backing Chairman Ángel Escribano's strategy to acquire a smaller competitor. This move aligns with Indra's efforts to expand its market presence and enhance its competitive edge in the industry. The acquisition plan is seen as a strategic step to bolster Indra's capabilities and growth potential.

data.title: Mexico's Debt Market Reaches Record Sales in February
data.description: Mexico's debt market has reached unprecedented levels this February, driven by Pemex's recent sale of $1.8 billion in local currency debt. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this transaction marks the largest ever in local markets. Despite being only halfway through the month, this sale has propelled monthly sales in Mexico to a new record. The significant transaction underscores the robust activity in the country's financial markets.

data.title: EU Moves to Ban All Crypto Transactions with Russian Entities: Report
data.description:
Lawmakers want to make it illegal for any party in the EU to transact with Russian counterparties using crypto, to help enforce sanctions.
source: https://decrypt.co/358159/eu-moves-to-ban-all-crypto-transactions-with-russian-entities-report

data.title: South Africa's Central Bank Plans to Replace Prime Rate with Benchmark Interest Rate
data.description:
South Africa's central bank is considering a shift in its approach to loan pricing by replacing the prime rate with its benchmark interest rate. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the central bank's intention to use the benchmark rate as the primary reference for commercial banks when pricing trillions of rands in loans. This move aims to streamline the financial system and enhance transparency in loan pricing.

The prime rate has long served as the standard for determining interest rates on loans in South Africa. However, the central bank believes that transitioning to the benchmark interest rate will provide a more accurate reflection of market conditions. This change is expected to impact various sectors, including housing, automotive, and personal loans, as banks adjust their pricing models.

The central bank's decision comes amid broader efforts to modernize the country's financial infrastructure and align it with international standards. By adopting the benchmark interest rate, South Africa aims to improve its competitiveness in the global market and attract foreign investment.

While the exact timeline for implementing this change has not been specified, the central bank is actively engaging with stakeholders to ensure a smooth transition. The move is anticipated to bring greater consistency and predictability to the lending environment, benefiting both borrowers and lenders.

As South Africa navigates this significant shift, the central bank remains committed to maintaining financial stability and supporting economic growth through effective monetary policy measures.

data.title: XRP Gains Attention Among Advisors and Clients
data.description: Advisors across the United States are frequently approached by clients with inquiries about XRP, making it one of the most discussed assets in the financial community, second only to Bitcoin. Grayscale posted on X. During Ripple’s XRP Community Day, it was highlighted that the interest in XRP remains strong, driven by a dedicated community and ongoing demand.
